description_long: This is a slender, elongated insect with a narrow, segmented body and long, spindly legs. Its coloration is primarily a mix of brown and beige, with darker markings along the thorax and lighter segments on the abdomen. The head is small and pointed, with two long antennae extending forward. The legs are thin and jointed, with the hind legs being the longest, giving it a stick-like appearance. The wings, if present, are not clearly visible, suggesting they may be folded or reduced. This insect resembles a type of assassin bug or water strider, commonly found in urban parks or near water sources.
description: A slender, brown and beige insect with long, spindly legs and a narrow, segmented body. It has a stick-like appearance and is likely an assassin bug or water strider.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full body length, segmented abdomen, and symmetrical leg arrangement.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the thin, flat body and the long, jointed legs extending outward.
description_view_2: Bottom-up view revealing the pale underside and the alignment of the legs and body segments.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, similar to view 1, showing the flatness and length of the body and legs.
